region_of_origin: Indian subcontinent; widely consumed in Singapore

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Chicken Sheekh Kebab is one skewer of approximately 100 g, comprising two to three pieces of minced chicken rolled and roasted on a skewer, commonly served with mint chutney and sliced onions.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Sheekh Kebab — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chicken Sheekh Kebab (cr_id G00010250). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on minced spiced meat kebab (low-carbohydrate grilled meat preparation)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Sheekh Kebab in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 30 (low) based on the analogous category of spiced minced meat kebab — a low-carbohydrate grilled meat preparation with 5.24 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (30 × 5.24) / 100 = 1.57, rounded to 1.6. This is consistent with the low-GI classification for high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled meat dishes documented in Atkinson et al. (2021).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg per 100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Chicken Sheekh Kebab due to its very high sodium content of 1080 mg/100 g; if consumed, request a reduced-salt preparation, avoid additional sauces or chutneys, and limit portion size to minimise blood pressure impact.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g) and low GI (~30) support glycaemic stability, but high fat content (26.4 g/100 g) may slow gastric emptying and alter post-meal glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chicken Sheekh Kebab is low in carbohydrate and has a low estimated glycaemic index, making it broadly suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the high fat content (26.4 g/100 g) may delay gastric emptying and affect post-prandial glucose — monitor blood glucose response and avoid sweet chutneys or dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg/100 g and estimated saturated fat ~5.5 g/100 g both elevate cardiovascular risk; total fat 26.4 g/100 g is also elevated
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat Chicken Sheekh Kebab as an occasional food only due to its high sodium (1080 mg/100 g) and elevated saturated fat (~5.5 g/100 g), both of which contribute to hypertension and atherogenic risk; a reduced-salt, lower-fat preparation is strongly recommended if consumed.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g RA threshold; estimated saturated fat ~5.5 g/100 g may promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Chicken Sheekh Kebab is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its very high sodium content (1080 mg/100 g), which worsens RA-related tissue swelling, and its saturated fat level (~5.5 g/100 g), which may trigger TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; a significantly reduced-salt preparation would be required before this dish could be considered RA-suitable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Sheekh Kebab is a South Asian grilled meat dish made from minced chicken blended with aromatic spices including cumin, coriander, garam masala, black pepper, ginger, garlic, and green chilli, then shaped around a metal or bamboo skewer and roasted over a flame or in a tandoor oven. The result is a firm, slightly charred, juicy cylinder of spiced minced chicken with a smoky, savoury flavour and a mildly spiced aroma. It is widely consumed across the Indian subcontinent and is a popular dish in Singapore's South Asian food culture, commonly served at lunch and dinner with mint chutney, sliced raw onions, and lemon wedges. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 302 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 26.4 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~5.5 g), and 5.24 g carbohydrate with 2.85 g sugar and 0 g dietary fibre. Sodium is notably high at 1080 mg/100 g, which is a significant clinical concern for individuals with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. The dish has a low estimated glycaemic index (~30) and is low in carbohydrate, making it broadly suitable for glycaemic management in Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without sweet accompaniments. The high fat content may slow gastric emptying and should be monitored in diabetic individuals. Potassium is 387 mg/100 g and calcium is 118 mg/100 g per the HPB Singapore database.

confidence_reason: Core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chicken Sheekh Kebab (cr_id G00010250) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at ~5.5 g/100 g based on typical chicken mince fat composition (consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for ground chicken). GI is an analogous estimate as no direct measurement exists in the University of Sydney GI Database for this specific dish.
notes: Saturated fat (5.5 g/100 g) is estimated from typical minced chicken fat composition cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (Ground chicken, raw, USDA SR Legacy); the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat. Dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database). The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed data for this specific Chicken Sheekh Kebab preparation are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026: monounsaturated_fat_g (-1): fatty acid fraction breakdown not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this exact preparation; polyunsaturated_fat_g (-1): fatty acid fraction breakdown not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this exact preparation; trans_fat_g (-1): trans fat not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; vitamin_c_mg (-1): vitamin panel not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; vitamin_a_mcg_rae (-1): vitamin panel not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (-1): vitamin panel not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (-1): vitamin panel not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (-1): vitamin panel not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; vitamin_b6_mg (-1): vitamin panel not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; vitamin_b12_mcg (-1): vitamin panel not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; folate_mcg_dfe (-1): folate not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; vitamin_d_mcg (-1): vitamin panel not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; vitamin_e_mg (-1): vitamin panel not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; phosphorus_mg (-1): mineral not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; zinc_mg (-1): mineral not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; selenium_mcg (-1): mineral not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish; water_g (-1): moisture content not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate based on spiced minced meat kebab category (low-carbohydrate grilled meat); no direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Sheekh Kebab in the University of Sydney GI Database. Halal status assumed based on chicken protein source and typical preparation; kosher status not confirmed. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean dish coverage (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Normalized input JSON to RESULT template structure; added multilingual names for Singaporean dish coverage; added health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease; added GI analogous estimate; added ontology anchors.
Iteration 2: Updated ontology match_type values from 'close' to 'closest_parent'; added documentation for all -1 fields in notes section to satisfy G3 numeric hygiene rule.

Iteration 3: Expanded notes field to individually document each -1 sentinel value with explicit reason and source consulted, satisfying the G3 requirement that each -1 field has its missing-data basis documented. All 17 fields set to -1 are now individually listed with reason: HPB Foodo does not report these fields for this dish and USDA FoodData Central does not have this exact preparation.
